```{r}
  "Friday morning",
  "Friday afternoon"
)
```

```{r}
arrival_long <- arrival_long %>%
  mutate(
    arrival_daytime = factor(
      arrival_daytime,
      levels = chronological_order
    )
  )
```

```{r}
arrival_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(
    x = arrival_daytime,
    y = CumulativePercent,
    color = year,
    group = year
  )) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Cumulative distribution of Census participant arrival times",
    x = "Day and time of arrival",
    y = "Cumulative percentage (%)",
    color = "Year"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5)
  )
```

```{r day time arrive table}
# arrival_combined %>% 
#   select(-last_col()) %>% 
#   kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
#       "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>Date and time of arrival</span>"
#     } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
#       "\\textbf{\\large Date and time of arrival}"
#     }) %>%
#   add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "2024" = 2)) %>% 
#   kable_classic() %>% 
#   kable_styling(latex_options = "H")
```
<br>

```{r transportation and RV}
# create df only containing transp and RV data
transpo_rv_25 <- data_2025 %>%
  select(arrive, rv)

arrive_25 <- table(transpo_rv_25$arrive)
arrive_25 <- as.data.frame(arrive_25)
colnames(arrive_25) <- c("Arrival", "Count")
arrive_25$Percent <- round((arrive_25$Count / sum(arrive_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)

rv_25 <- table(transpo_rv_25$rv)
rv_25 <- as.data.frame(rv_25)
colnames(rv_25) <- c("Sleeping", "Count")
rv_25$Percent <- round((rv_25$Count / sum(rv_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

### Transportation Method

```{r transportation pie chart}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_transpo <- arrive_25 %>% 
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
arrive_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = "", 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Arrival))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_transpo,
                   aes(y = pos, 
                       label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, 
                   nudge_x = 1, 
                   show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Question: How did you arrive?")) +
  ggtitle("\n\nTransportation method: Most participants carpooled") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(arrive_25$Count), "participants, including omitted responses \n")) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5))
```

```{r longitudinal transportation}
# # Create a new row with NA values
# new_row <- data.frame(Arrival = NA, Count = NA, Percent = NA)
# 
# # Insert the new row at the second index
# arrive <- rbind(arrive[1:2, ], new_row, arrive[-(1:2), ])
```

```{r combine arrive}
arrive_df <- cbind(arrive, arrive_24)

colnames(arrive_df)[1] <- "Transportation method"
colnames(arrive_df)[2] <- "2023 Count"
colnames(arrive_df)[3] <- "2023 Percent"
colnames(arrive_df)[4] <- "Transportation method 2024"
colnames(arrive_df)[5] <- "2024 Count"
colnames(arrive_df)[6] <- "2024 Percent"
```

```{r}
arrival_combined <- arrive_df %>%
  left_join(
    arrive_25,
    by = c("Transportation method 2024" = "Arrival")
  )
```

```{r}
colnames(arrival_combined)[7] <- "2025 Count"
colnames(arrival_combined)[8] <- "2025 Percent"
```

```{r create cumulative carpool totals}
# Calculate totals for carpooled
total_carpooled <- arrival_combined %>%
  filter(grepl("carpooled", 
               `Transportation method`, 
               ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  summarize(
    `Transportation method` = "Total carpooled",
    `2023 Count` = sum(`2023 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2023
    `2023 Percent` = sum(`2023 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Transportation method 2024` = "Total carpooled",
    `2024 Count` = sum(`2024 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2024
    `2024 Percent` = sum(`2024 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `2025 Count` = sum(`2025 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2025
    `2025 Percent` = sum(`2025 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Calculate totals for drove solo
total_drove_solo <- arrival_combined %>%
  filter(grepl("drove", 
               `Transportation method`, 
               ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  summarize(
    `Transportation method` = "Total drove solo",
    `2023 Count` = sum(`2023 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2023
    `2023 Percent` = sum(`2023 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Transportation method 2024` = "Total drove solo",
    `2024 Count` = sum(`2024 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2024
    `2024 Percent` = sum(`2024 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `2025 Count` = sum(`2025 Count`, na.rm = TRUE),   # Raw count for 2025
    `2025 Percent` = sum(`2025 Percent`, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Combine original data with totals
arrival_combined <- bind_rows(arrival_combined, 
                       total_carpooled, 
                       total_drove_solo)
```

```{r reshape arrival}
spaghetti_data <- arrival_combined %>%
  filter(`Transportation method` %in% c("Total carpooled", "Total drove solo")) %>%
  select(`Transportation method`, `2023 Percent`, `2024 Percent`, `2025 Percent`)

# Reshape the data to long format for ggplot
spaghetti_data_long <- spaghetti_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`2023 Percent`, `2024 Percent`, `2025 Percent`),
    names_to = "Year",
    values_to = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Year = sub(" Percent", "", Year))
```

```{r longitudinal arrival}
# Create the spaghetti plot
spaghetti_data_long %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, 
             y = Percent, 
             color = `Transportation method`, 
             group = `Transportation method`)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +    # Line for each group
  geom_point(size = 3) +    # Points at each year
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Total carpooled" = "darkgreen",
      "Total drove solo" = "grey"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "How did you arrive at SOAK?",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Total participants who \n carpooled or drove solo (%)",
    color = "Transportation Method"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.1, 0.5), "cm"),  # Tighter plot margin
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
        legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}
arrival_combined <- arrival_combined %>% 
  arrange(desc(`2025 Percent`))
```

```{r}
colnames(arrival_combined) <- c("Transportation method", 
                         "Count", "Percent", 
                         "Transportation method", 
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r}
arrival_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>How did you arrive at SOAK?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large How did you arrive at SOAK?}"
    }, 
    row.names = FALSE) %>% 
  add_header_above(c("2023" = 3, 
                     "2024" = 3,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>%  # Ensure 6 columns (3 for 2023, 2 for 2024)
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```
<br>

The majority (67.5%) of participants carpooled to SOAK, although this was a decrease from 2023 and 2024. Vehicle passes were instituted in 2024, but there was an increase in those who drove solo for each year since. This is more than Burning Man, where [in 2024 only 19.2% of Burners drove solo](https://blackrockcitycensus.org/travel#how-many-people-were-in-your-vehicle-when-you-arrived-at-the-gate).

### Sleeping Arrangement and RV Opinions

```{r RV pie chart}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_rv <- rv_25 %>% 
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
rv_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Sleeping))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_rv,
                   aes(y = pos, 
                       label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, 
                   nudge_x = 1, 
                   show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Response")) +
  ggtitle("\n\n\nWill you be staying in an RV or camping vehicle?") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(rv_25$Count), "participants, including omitted responses \n")) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5))
```

```{r}
colnames(rv_25)[1] <- "Response"
```

```{r longitudinal camping vehicles}
rv_combined <- merge(
  rv_combined,
  rv_25,
  by = "Response",
  all = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
names(rv_combined) <- make.unique(names(rv_combined))
```

```{r long rv}
# Reshape the data into long format
rv_long <- rv_combined %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("Percent.x", "Percent.x.1", "Percent.y"),
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = recode(
      year,
      "Percent.x"   = "2023",
      "Percent.x.1" = "2024",
      "Percent.y"   = "2025"
    )
  )
```

``` {r longitudinal rv plot}
# Create spaghetti plot
rv_long %>% 
  filter(Response != "Omitted") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, 
             y = percent, 
             group = Response, 
             color = Response)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Optional, to emphasize points
  labs(title = "Are you staying in an RV?",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Total particpants (%)",
       color = "Response") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.2, 0)) +  # Control x-axis padding
    theme(
          plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.1, 0.5), "cm"),  # Tighter plot margin
          axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.5),   # Adjust x-axis label spacing
          axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 0.5),    # Adjust y-axis label spacing
          axis.ticks.length = unit(-0.15, "cm"),            # Move ticks closer to axis
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
          panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"))
```

```{r}
rv_combined <- rv_combined %>% 
  arrange(desc(Percent.y))
```

```{r rv renaming}
colnames(rv_combined) <- c("Response", 
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r rv table}
rv_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>Will you be staying in an RV or camping vehicle?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large Will you be staying in an RV or camping vehicle?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```
<br>

This question bakes in an opinion in addition to gaining information on how participants sleep at SOAK. Only 12.4% of participants stayed in an RV or camping vehicle, which is an increase from the previous year. Of those who did not stay in RVs, most did not have an opinion on their presence at SOAK.

### Ticket Acquisition

```{r ticketing 2025}
#count occurrences
ticket_counts_25 <- table(data_2025$ticket)

#convert the result to a data frame
tickets_25 <- data.frame(tickets = names(ticket_counts_25), 
                         count = as.numeric(ticket_counts_25))
colnames(tickets_25) <- c("Ticket", "Count")
tickets_25$Percent <- round((tickets_25$Count / sum(tickets_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

```{r ticket plot}
#plot ticket sales
tickets_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Ticket, 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = Ticket)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
           color = "black") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PRGn") + 
  labs(
    x = "Acquisition method",
    y = "Participants (%)",
    title = "\nHow did you acquire your SOAK ticket?") +
   geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percent)), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), 
            size = 3.5, color = "black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5)) +
  guides(fill = FALSE)
```

```{r tix for table}
tix_combined <- merge(
  tix_combined,
  tickets_25,
  by = "Ticket",
  all = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
names(tix_combined) <- make.unique(names(tix_combined))
```

```{r}
tix_combined <- tix_combined %>% 
  arrange(desc(Percent.y))
```

```{r}
colnames(tix_combined) <- c("Response", 
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r the table}
tix_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>How did you acquire your SOAK ticket?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large How did you acquire your SOAK ticket?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```

The above table is organized in descending order of 2025 Percent values. Ticket acquisition for SOAK remained mostly done through directed group sales (DGS) at 30% in 2025, followed by purchased through the main sale at 19.9%. In 2025 fewer folks obtained their ticket through paying back their friend, which was the 2nd most common way to get a ticket in 2024.

Subsidized tickets (Burning Man's version of the ticket aid program) accounted for 3.6%.

### SOAK Location

```{r move}
#counting occurrences 2025
move_counts_25 <- table(data_2025$move)

#convert the result to a data frame
move_25 <- data.frame(move = names(move_counts_25), count = as.numeric(move_counts_25))

colnames(move_25) <- c("Move", "Count")
move_25$Percent <- round((move_25$Count / sum(move_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

```{r}
names(move_combined) <- make.unique(names(move_combined))
```

```{r}
move_combined <- move_combined %>%
  left_join(
    move_25,
    by = c("Response" = "Move")
  )
```

```{r move chart}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_move <- move_25 %>% 
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
move_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = "", 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Move))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_move,
                   aes(y = pos, 
                       label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, 
                   nudge_x = 1, 
                   show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Response")) +
  ggtitle("\nWould you attend SOAK if it moved location?") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(move_25$Count), "participants, including omitted responses \n")) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5))
```

```{r}
colnames(move_combined) <- c("Response", 
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r}
move_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>Would you attended SOAK if it moved location?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large Would you attended SOAK if it moved location?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```

Most participants (60%) would follow SOAK if it moved. Cautiously, 37.1% might attend SOAK if it moved. Perhaps location-dependent? Only a small fraction, 1.3%, claimed they would not attend SOAK if it left Justesen Ranch in Tygh Valley. These values are relatively consistent since 2023.

### Participation

```{r participation 2025}
# rows (sometimes) store several responses
# parse them out
participate_list_25 <- strsplit(data_2025$participate, ",")
participate_unlist_25 <- unlist(participate_list_25)
party_occurrences_25 <- table(participate_unlist_25)
participate_df_25 <- as.data.frame(party_occurrences_25)

# volunteering as soak lead, director, or producer were categorized separately due to commas - combine
colnames(participate_df_25) <- c("Participation", "Count")

# manually replacing value with original full value
participate_df_25$Participation <- as.character(participate_df_25$Participation)
participate_df_25[9,1] <- "Volunteering as SOAK lead, director, or producer"

# add rows that were combined
participate_df_25[9,2] <- sum(participate_df_25$Count[c(1, 2, 9)])

# drop rows 1 and 2
participate_df_25 <- participate_df_25[-c(1, 2), ]

participate_df_25$Percent <- round(participate_df_25$Count / 914 * 100, 1)
```

```{r part graph}
participate_df_25$Participation <- factor(participate_df_25$Participation,
                                       levels = participate_df_25$Participation[order(participate_df_25$Count, 
                                                                                      decreasing = TRUE)])

participate_df_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Participation,
             y = Count,
             fill = Participation)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           color = "black") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG") +
  labs(x = "How participants participate",
       y = "Number of times reported",
       title = "\nHow will you participate this year?") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), 
            size = 3.5, 
            color = "black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                    fill = NA, 
                                    size = 0.5)) +
  guides(fill = FALSE)
```

```{r}
names(part_combined) <- make.unique(names(part_combined))
```

```{r move combined}
part_combined <- part_combined %>%
  left_join(
    participate_df_25,
    by = c("Response" = "Participation")
  )
```

```{r long participation}
# Reshape the data into long format
part_long <- part_combined %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Percent.x", 
                        "Percent.1", 
                        "Percent.y"), 
               names_to = "year", 
               values_to = "percent") %>%
  mutate(year = recode(year, 
                       "Percent.x" = "2023", 
                       "Percent.1" = "2024",
                       "Percent.y" = "2025"))
```

```{r part long graph}
part_long %>% 
  filter(Response != "Omitted") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, 
             y = percent, 
             group = Response, 
             color = Response,
             shape = Response)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Optional, to emphasize points
  labs(title = "How will you participate this year?",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Total particpants (%)",
       color = "Response",
       shape = "Response") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.2, 0)) +  # Control x-axis padding
    theme(
          plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.1, 0.5), "cm"),  # Tighter plot margin
          axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.5),   # Adjust x-axis label spacing
          axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 0.5),    # Adjust y-axis label spacing
          axis.ticks.length = unit(-0.15, "cm"),            # Move ticks closer to axis
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
          panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"))
```

```{r}
part_combined <- part_combined %>% 
  arrange(desc(Percent.y))
```

```{r}
colnames(part_combined) <- c("Response", 
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent",
                         "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r}
part_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>How will you participate this year?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large How will you participate this year?}"
    }, 
    row.names = FALSE) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```
<br>

How did you participate at SOAK in 2025? Participants could select more than one option, therefore the percent here is out of participants and not responses (totaling above 100). Most people helped bring a theme camp! A total of 57.1% reported bringing a theme camp, which is an increase from 2024 and more aligned with the responses in 2023. Second to bringing a camp, 48.4% of people reported interacting with art or theme camps, which increased the last 2 years.

The least common forms of participation were volunteering as a lead, director, producer, or board member. [Volunteerism in Black Rock City claimed 13.5% of Burners in 2024](https://blackrockcitycensus.org/regionals_media_volunteering#volunteered-in-the-last-year), but 21.9% of SOAKers volunteered with 1 or more SOAK department. This is a decrease from 2024, where 27.6% of participants volunteered. Taken together with ticket acquisition method, more folks are volunteering and obtaining a gift ticket through their volunteer hours year to year.

### Sustainability

```{r}
# rows (sometimes) store several responses
# parse them out
sustain_list <- strsplit(data_2025$sustain, ",")
sustain_unlist <- unlist(sustain_list)
sustain_occurrences25 <- table(sustain_unlist)
sustain_df25 <- as.data.frame(sustain_occurrences25)
```

```{r}
sustain_df25 <- sustain_df25 %>%
  mutate(
    sustain_unlist = fct_collapse(
      sustain_unlist,
      Omitted = c("Omitted", "Decline to answer")
    )
  )
```

```{r}
sustain_df25 <- sustain_df25 %>%
  group_by(sustain_unlist) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = sum(Freq, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
#be mindful of hard coding here
colnames(sustain_df25) <- c("Sustainability", "Count")
sustain_df25$Percent <- round(sustain_df25$Count / 914 * 100, 1)
```

```{r sustain plot}
sustain_df25$Sustainability <- factor(sustain_df25$Sustainability,
                                    levels = sustain_df25$Sustainability[order(sustain_df25$Percent,
                                                                             decreasing = TRUE)])
sustain_df25 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Sustainability,
             y = Percent,
             fill = Sustainability)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           color = "black") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlGn") +
  labs(x = "Sustainability method",
       y = "Percent of surveyed individuals",
       title = "\nHow are you contributing to sustainability at SOAK?") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", 
                                Percent)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3.5, 
            color = "black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1)) +
  guides(fill = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# merge the two tables
sustain_combined <- merge(
  sustain_combined,
  sustain_df25,
  by = "Sustainability",
  all = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
names(sustain_combined) <- make.unique(names(sustain_combined))
```

```{r}
sustain_combined <- sustain_combined  %>%
  arrange(desc(Percent.y))
```

```{r}
colnames(sustain_combined) <- c("Sustainability", 
                                "Count", "Percent", 
                                "Count", "Percent",
                                "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r sustain table}
sustain_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>How are you contributing to sustainability?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large How are you contributing to sustainability?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```

### Inclusion of Minors

```{r minors 2025}
# create df only containing bringing kids data
kids25 <- data_2025 %>%
  select(kids, teens)

kids_counts <- table(kids25$kids)
teens_counts <- table(kids25$teens)

#convert the result to a data frame
kids_25 <- data.frame(kids = names(kids_counts), count = as.numeric(kids_counts))
teens_25 <- data.frame(teens = names(teens_counts), count = as.numeric(teens_counts))

# Replace "Omitted" with 0 for easier handling
kids_25$kids <- as.character(kids_25$kids) # Ensure 'kids' is treated as a character column
kids_25$kids[kids_25$kids == "Omitted"] <- "0"
```

```{r}
# Create a new column categorizing "Yes" and "No/Omitted"
kids_25$category <- ifelse(kids_25$kids == 0, "No/Omitted", "Yes")
```

```{r}
# Summarize the counts by category
kids_25_simple <- kids_25 %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(Count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Rename columns to match your desired output
kids_25_simple <- kids_25_simple %>%
  rename(Kids = category)
```

```{r}
kids_25_simple$Percent <- round((kids_25_simple$Count / sum(kids_25_simple$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

```{r teens 24}
# Replace "Omitted" with 0 for easier handling
teens_25$teens <- as.character(teens_25$teens)
teens_25$teens[teens_25$teens == "Omitted"] <- "0"

# Create a new column categorizing "Yes" and "No/Omitted"
teens_25$category <- ifelse(teens_25$teens == 0, "No/Omitted", "Yes")
```

```{r}
# Summarize the counts by category
teens_25_simple <- teens_25 %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(Count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Rename columns to match your desired output
teens_25_simple <- teens_25_simple %>%
  rename(Teens = category)
```

```{r counting participants who brought teens}
teens_25_simple$Percent <- round((teens_25_simple$Count / sum(teens_25_simple$Count) * 100), 
                                 digits = 1)
```

```{r minors graphed}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_kids <- kids_25_simple %>%
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
kids_25_simple %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Kids))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PiYG") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_kids,
                   aes(y = pos, label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, nudge_x = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Brought children")) +
  ggtitle("\nHow many participants reported bringing any children") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(kids_25_simple$Count), "participants, omitted and 0 responses combined \n")) +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_teens <- teens_25_simple %>%
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
teens_25_simple %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = Percent, fill = fct_inorder(Teens))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PRGn") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_teens,
                   aes(y = pos, label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, nudge_x = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Brought teenagers")) +
  ggtitle("\nHow many participants reported bringing any teenagers") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(teens_25_simple$Count), "participants, omitted and 0 responses combined\n")) +
  theme_void()
```

```{r total minors brought to soak}
kids_25 <- kids_25 %>%
  mutate(
    kids_n = case_when(
      kids == "0" ~ 0,
      kids == "1" ~ 1,
      kids == "2" ~ 2,
      kids == "3" ~ 3,
      kids == "4 or more children" ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# Filter rows with category == "Yes" and calculate totals for kids
kids_total <- sum(
  kids_25$kids_n[kids_25$category == "Yes"] * 
    kids_25$count[kids_25$category == "Yes"],
  na.rm = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
teens_25 <- teens_25 %>%
  mutate(
    teens_n = case_when(
      teens == "0" ~ 0,
      teens == "1" ~ 1,
      teens == "2" ~ 2,
      teens == "3" ~ 3,
      teens == "4 or more teenagers" ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# Filter rows with category == "Yes" and calculate totals for teens
teens_total <- sum(
  teens_25$teens_n[teens_25$category == "Yes"] * 
    teens_25$count[teens_25$category == "Yes"],
  na.rm = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
# Create the minors_counted data frame
minors_counted <- data.frame(
  kids_total = kids_total,
  teens_total = teens_total
)
```

```{r longitudinal yes minors}
longitudinal_minors <- data.frame(
  Year = rep(c(2023, 2024, 2025), 
             each = 2),
  Category = rep(c("Children", 
                   "Teens"), 
                 times = 3),
  Percent = c(
    kids_2$Percent[kids_2$Kids == "Yes"],
    teens$Percent[teens$Teens == "Yes"],
    kids_24_simple$Percent[kids_24_simple$Kids == "Yes"],
    teens_24_simple$Percent[teens_24_simple$Teens == "Yes"],
    kids_25_simple$Percent[kids_25_simple$Kids == "Yes"],
    teens_25_simple$Percent[teens_25_simple$Teens == "Yes"]
  )
)
```

```{r}
# Create the longitudinal plot
longitudinal_minors %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, 
             y = Percent, 
             group = Category, 
             color = Category)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2023, 2024, 2025)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Children" = "#87CEFA", 
                                "Teens" = "#FFA07A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Participants who reported bringing minors",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Percent",
    color = "Category") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    theme(
          plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.1, 0.5), "cm"),  # Tighter plot margin
          axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.5),   # Adjust x-axis label spacing
          axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 0.5),    # Adjust y-axis label spacing
          axis.ticks.length = unit(-0.15, "cm"),            # Move ticks closer to axis
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
          panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"))
```

Most participants do not bring minors with them to SOAK. Compared to Burning Man in 2024, [5.2% of Burners reported ever bringing someone under 18 years of age](https://blackrockcitycensus.org/perspectives#bringing-children-under-18-years-old), SOAKers have a higher rate of brining minors. 

```{r}
# # Convert minors_counted to a data frame suitable for plotting
# minors_plot <- data.frame(
#   Category = c("Children", "Teens"),
#   Total = c(minors_counted$kids_total, 
#             minors_counted$teens_total)
# )
# 
# # Create the bar plot
# ggplot(minors_plot, aes(x = Category, 
#                         y = Total, 
#                         fill = Category)) +
#   geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
#            color = "black", 
#            width = 0.6) +
#   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Children" = "#87CEFA", 
#                                "Teens" = "#FFA07A")) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = Total), 
#             vjust = 2.5, 
#             color = "black", 
#             size = 4) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Total counts of minors reportedly brought by participants",
#     x = "Age group",
#     y = "Total count",
#     fill = "Category"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(
#     plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
#     legend.position = "none" # Remove legend
#   )
```

## Town Hall and Burning Man

### Town Hall

```{r townhall}
#counting occurrences
townhall_counts_25 <- table(data_2025$townhall)

#convert the result to a data frame
townhall_25 <- data.frame(move = names(townhall_counts_25), 
                          count = as.numeric(townhall_counts_25))

colnames(townhall_25) <- c("Response", "Count")
townhall_25$Percent <- round((townhall_25$Count / sum(townhall_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

```{r}
names(townhall_combined) <- make.unique(names(townhall_combined))
```

```{r townhall longitudinal}
# combine townhall dfs
townhall_combined <- merge(
  townhall_combined,
  townhall_25,
  by = "Response",
  all = TRUE
)
```

```{r town pie}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_town <- townhall_25 %>% 
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

#the plot
townhall_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = "", 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Response))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu") +
  geom_label_repel(data = pie_labels_town,
                   aes(y = pos, 
                       label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
                   size = 4.5, 
                   nudge_x = 1, 
                   show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Question: Did you attend Town Hall?")) +
  ggtitle("\nMost participants did not attend Town Hall ") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(townhall_25$Count), "participants, including omitted responses \n")) +
  theme_void() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", 
                                      fill = NA, 
                                      size = 0.5))
```

```{r}
colnames(townhall_combined) <- c("Response", 
                                "Count", "Percent", 
                                "Count", "Percent",
                                "Count", "Percent")
```
<br>
```{r}
townhall_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>Did you attend Town Hall?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large Did you attend Town Hall?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```
<br>

Only 6.1% of people attended SOAK's Town Hall in February, 2025, which is more than in 2024. Most people reported not attending (80.1%), followed by a lack of knowing what Town Hall is (12.9%). This year more people knew what Town Hall was but still did not attend.

### Burning Man

```{r bm}
#counting occurrences 2025
bm_counts_25<- table(data_2025$bm)

#convert the result to a data frame
burning_man_25 <- data.frame(move = names(bm_counts_25), count = as.numeric(bm_counts_25))

colnames(burning_man_25) <- c("BM", "Count")
burning_man_25$Percent <- round((burning_man_25$Count / sum(burning_man_25$Count) * 100), digits = 1)
```

```{r}
names(bm_combined) <- make.unique(names(bm_combined))

colnames(burning_man_25) <- c("Response", "Count", "Percent")

# combine bm dfs
bm_combined <- merge(bm_combined,
                     burning_man_25,
                     by = "Response",
                     all = TRUE
                     )
```

```{r}
# rename the columns
bm_combined <- bm_combined %>%
  rename("2023 Percent" = Percent.x, 
         "2024 Percent" = Percent.1, 
         "2025 Percent" = Percent.y) %>%
  select(
    `Response`,
    `2023 Percent`, 
    `2024 Percent`,
    `2025 Percent`)
```

```{r burning pie}
#maths for pie chart
pie_labels_bm <- burning_man_25 %>% 
  mutate(csum = rev(cumsum(rev(Percent))),
         pos = Percent/2 + lead(csum, 1),
         pos = if_else(is.na(pos), Percent/2, pos))

burning_man_25 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = "", 
             y = Percent, 
             fill = fct_inorder(Response))) +
  geom_col(width = 1, 
           color = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BuPu") +
  geom_text(data = pie_labels_bm,
            aes(y = pos, 
                label = paste0(Percent, "%")),
            size = 4.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Question: Do you plan \n to go to Burning Man this year?")) +
  ggtitle("\nMost participants did not plan to go to Burning Man ") +
  labs(caption = paste(sum(burning_man_25$Count), "participants, including omitted responses \n")) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# colnames(bm_combined)[1] <- "Response"
# colnames(bm_combined)[4] <- "Count"
# colnames(bm_combined)[5] <- "Percent"
```

```{r}
bm_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>Do you plan to attend Burning Man this year?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large Do you plan to attend Burning Man this year?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 1, 
                     "2024" = 1,
                     "2025" = 1)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```

The majority of participants (62.1%) did not plan to go to Burning Man in 2025, followed by 23.9% who planned to go.

### Communication

```{r}
# create df only containing communication data
comm25 <- data_2025 %>%
  select(info)
```

```{r}
# Clean and standardize the data
comm25 <- comm25 %>%
  mutate(
    info = info %>%
      trimws() %>%                             # Remove leading/trailing spaces
      gsub(" +", " ", .)                   # Remove multiple spaces
  )
```

```{r}
# rows (sometimes) store several responses
# parse them out
comm_list <- strsplit(comm25$info, ",")
comm_unlist <- unlist(comm_list)
comm_occurrences25 <- table(comm_unlist)
comm_df25 <- as.data.frame(comm_occurrences25)

colnames(comm_df25) <- c("Information", 
                         "Count")
comm_df25$Percent <- round(comm_df25$Count / sum(comm_df25$Count) * 100, 1)
```

```{r}
comm_df25$Information <- factor(comm_df25$Information,
                                    levels = comm_df25$Information[order(comm_df25$Percent,
                                                                             decreasing = TRUE)])
comm_df25 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Information,
             y = Count,
             fill = Information)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           color = "black") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  labs(x = "Information method",
       y = "Number of times reported",
       title = "\nHow participants received SOAK related information") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3.5, color = "black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  guides(fill = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# merge the two tables
comm_combined <- merge(comm_combined, comm_df25, 
                       by = "Information", 
                       all = TRUE)
```

```{r}
names(comm_combined) <- make.unique(names(comm_combined))
```

```{r}
comm_combined <- comm_combined  %>%
  arrange(desc(Percent.y))
```

```{r}
colnames(comm_combined) <- c("Information", 
                             "Count", "Percent", 
                             "Count", "Percent",
                             "Count", "Percent")
```

```{r comm table}
comm_combined %>% 
  kbl(caption = if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
      "<span style='font-size: 16px;'>How did you receive SOAK related information?</span>"
    } else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      "\\textbf{\\large How did you receive SOAK related information?}"
    }) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, 
                     "2023" = 2, 
                     "2024" = 2,
                     "2025" = 2)) %>% 
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE) %>% 
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", 
                      "scale_down"),
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5cm")
```

SOAK's mailing list and website are how most folks receive SOAK information. 

```{r}
save.image("C:/Users/Amanda Welch/Desktop/SOAK/SOAK23_24_25.RData")
```
